budget_wines.name: Donnafugata Sedara Nero d'Avola Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Donnafugata enoteca in Marsala; widely distributed through Italian and international retailers including Vivino-listed shops across the UK, Germany, USA and Japan.
budget_wines.description: Donnafugata's entry-level Nero d'Avola from Sicilian DOC vineyards. Ripe cherry, carob and warm spice; the approachable house style that introduces Donnafugata's range. Drink young.
budget_wines.tip: Sedara is reliably available and under EUR 15 in major Italian supermarkets and wine shops. A reliable introduction to Nero d'Avola at near-everyday pricing.

budget_wines.name: Planeta Cerasuolo di Vittoria DOCG
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Planeta estate shop at Feudo Dispensa, Menfi; distributed through major Italian retailers and exported internationally via importer Winebow in the USA.
budget_wines.description: Planeta's Cerasuolo di Vittoria from their Vittoria estate, blending Nero d'Avola with Frappato in the DOCG's classic ratio. Red cherry, pomegranate and violet; lighter and more elegant than pure Nero d'Avola bottlings.
budget_wines.tip: Sicily's only DOCG blend at under EUR 20 represents exceptional value for the appellation tier. Serve slightly cool at 16 C with grilled swordfish or arancini.

budget_wines.name: COS Frappato Terre Siciliane IGT
budget_wines.where_to_buy: COS estate, Strada Provinciale 3, Vittoria; distributed by Indie Wineries in Italy and by importer Rosenthal Wine Merchant in the USA.
budget_wines.description: COS's entry-level varietal Frappato from organic farming in Vittoria, fermented in amphora and concrete with native yeasts. Sour cherry, hibiscus and white pepper; one of Sicily's most sippable natural reds. Light-bodied and best served cool.
budget_wines.tip: The COS Frappato is as close as Sicily gets to light-bodied Beaujolais in weight and style. Ideal for warm evenings with cured meats and caciocavallo.

budget_wines.name: Tasca d'Almerita Regaleali Bianco Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Tasca d'Almerita estate, Regaleali, Valledolmo; distributed across major Italian retailers and USA importer selected by Banfi Vintners.
budget_wines.description: The flagship entry-level white from the Regaleali estate in the Sicilian interior. A blend of native white varieties with fresh citrus, white almond and floral character. Drink within two years of vintage for its freshest expression.
budget_wines.tip: Regaleali Bianco is one of the most reliably fresh and well-made Sicilian whites at entry-level pricing. Look for it at any decent Italian enoteca under EUR 14.

budget_wines.name: Marco de Bartoli Pignatello Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Marco de Bartoli estate, Contrada Fornaia, Marsala; distributed in Italy and USA through importer Neal Rosenthal Wine Merchant.
budget_wines.description: A varietal Perricone (locally known as Pignatello) from Marco de Bartoli's Sicilian estate. Deep ruby, dried cherry, bitter chocolate and bay leaf; an indigenous variety rarely bottled solo and virtually unknown outside Sicily. Minimal intervention, no fining.
budget_wines.tip: Perricone is one of Sicily's most distinctive and underexposed red varieties. The de Bartoli bottling is the accessible entry point; seek it out at quality Italian enoteche.

budget_wines.name: Duca di Salaparuta Corvo Bianco Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Duca di Salaparuta winery, Casteldaccia; available in most Italian supermarkets and large wine shops across Italy. Exported to over 50 countries.
budget_wines.description: Corvo Bianco is one of Sicily's most widely distributed whites, a fresh, clean Catarratto-Inzolia blend from Duca di Salaparuta. Light citrus, melon and crisp acidity. Entry-level and widely available; benchmark approachable Sicilian white at everyday pricing.
budget_wines.tip: Found at virtually every Italian supermarket and exported widely; the most reliably available Sicilian white under EUR 10 internationally.

budget_wines.name: Feudi del Pisciotto Grillo Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Feudi del Pisciotto estate, Niscemi; distributed by Moretti Wines in Italy and selected UK wine merchants.
budget_wines.description: Single-varietal Grillo from the Feudi del Pisciotto estate in the Niscemi area of central-south Sicily. Broad citrus peel, salted almond and a briny mineral finish characteristic of inland Grillo planted far from the sea. Well-priced for the variety and the estate's profile.
budget_wines.tip: Grillo in this area of Sicily has a distinct textured character different from the lighter coastal examples; pair with pasta alla Norma or grilled aubergine.

budget_wines.name: Morgante Nero d'Avola Classico Sicilia DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Morgante estate, Grotte (Agrigento province); distributed by Enoteca Italiana and major Italian wine chains. Exported to USA through a New York importer.
budget_wines.description: Morgante's Classico is the entry tier from one of Agrigento province's benchmark Nero d'Avola producers. The Classico shows the variety's signature dark cherry, carob and sun-dried tomato character at a price well below the more aged Don Antonio riserva tier.
budget_wines.tip: Morgante's vineyards in Grotte are at higher elevation than most Sicilian DOC plantings, giving the Classico more freshness and acidity than typical Nero d'Avola.

budget_wines.name: Benanti Etna Bianco DOC
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Benanti estate, Viagrande; distributed in Italy through Enoteca Properzio and Enoteca Italiana. Exported USA via Skurnik Wines.
budget_wines.description: Benanti's estate-level Etna Bianco is the benchmark for the Carricante variety at entry price. Volcanic minerality, green citrus, fennel and a saline finish. Made from pre-phylloxera alberello vines on the eastern Etna slopes, this is the most accessible and affordable Benanti white.
budget_wines.tip: At EUR 18-22, the Benanti Etna Bianco is the most price-efficient way to taste the volcanic character of Carricante on its own appellation, without paying premium-contrada pricing.

budget_wines.name: Arianna Occhipinti SP68 Bianco Terre Siciliane IGT
budget_wines.where_to_buy: Arianna Occhipinti estate, Vittoria; distributed by De Maison Selections in the USA and Caves de Pyrene in the UK.
budget_wines.description: Arianna Occhipinti's SP68 Bianco is a blend of Albanello and Zibibbo (Muscat of Alexandria) from organic farming near Vittoria. Pale gold, fragrant orange blossom, salted lemon peel and a textured, naturally fermented finish. One of Sicily's most individual and distinctive under-EUR 25 white wines.
budget_wines.tip: SP68 Bianco is named after a Vittoria-area road, not a numbered vintage. Low-sulfur and alive; drink within two years of vintage and serve well chilled.
